description: About the Company Locum Apps is Malaysia's leading healthcare staffing platform, connecting credentialed clinicians with hospital partners across five cities. Backed by Iterative and a WSA Global Winner 2024, we are scaling toward regional expansion and a Series A raise. Key Responsibilities 1. Product & Feature Implementation Implement internal tools, dashboards, and platform features in close collaboration with the engineering team. Translate founder and operations team needs into working technical specs and, where possible, working code. Support the scoping and delivery of product features across the Locum Apps platform. Work closely with the tech team to ensure new implementations are aligned with existing architecture and standards. 2. Data & Operational Reporting Own operational reporting covering job volumes, clinician activity, hospital engagement, and platform health metrics. Build and maintain data pipelines that support accurate, timely internal reporting. Support data hygiene, structuring, and documentation as the company prepares for institutional fundraising. Prepare data reports on a daily, weekly, and monthly basis as assigned by Management. 3. Workflow Automation Identify manual workflows across operations and growth functions and implement automation solutions. Build and maintain automated triggers, alerts, and recurring task systems to reduce reliance on human follow-up. Continuously improve existing automation for reliability and scalability. 4. Cross-Functional Support Work closely with the Growth and Operations teams to understand data needs and translate them into actionable outputs. Gather feedback from internal teams to improve reporting tools and internal systems. Stay informed on industry trends, competitor activity, and relevant technology developments in the healthcare and staffing sectors. What We Are Looking For: 1–3 years in a backend development role — you write real code, ship to production, and own what you build Proficient in at least one backend language (Python, Node.js, or equivalent) and comfortable working with APIs and databases Strong SQL skills; able to build and maintain data pipelines without hand-holding Enough product instinct to distinguish a good spec from a vague one Operates well in ambiguity — you figure things out, then figure out a better way Healthcare, marketplace, or startup experience is a plus, not a requirement
